title: Harden a release/publish pipeline
description: Add environment protection, provenance, and least-privilege publish so a tag push cannot silently ship malware.
body: Steps: 1. Move the publish step into a job bound to a protected environment with required reviewers. 2. Scope GITHUB_TOKEN/registry creds to packages:write only on that job. 3. Generate build provenance/attestation (SLSA) and verify it on consume. 4. Ensure the publish trigger is a signed tag from a protected branch, not arbitrary push. 5. Confirm the published artifact hash matches the built artifact. Rollback point: yank/deprecate the published version and revert the workflow. Validation: a publish requires environment approval and produces a verifiable provenance record tied to the source commit.
